contradictions.topic: Accelerated stability data extrapolation for biologicals
contradictions.skill_1_instruction: Q1E extrapolation tables allow shelf life extension based on accelerated data results (Phase 3, Section 3.2)
contradictions.skill_2_instruction: Q5C states accelerated data NOT relied upon for extrapolation for biologicals due to physical sensitivity (BS-015 context; Phase 4)
contradictions.resolution: Not a true contradiction. skill-stability-analysis.md covers chemical entities; skill-biotech-quality.md covers biologicals. Both are correct for their respective product types. The 2025 draft section (7.7) in stability skill also notes biologicals extrapolation is limited to 1.5x data.
contradictions.risk: LOW — Only a problem if a user applies the stability skill to a biological without also consulting the biotech quality skill

contradictions.skill_1_instruction: skill-regulatory-compliance.md Level 3 FDA: 'BCS biowaiver: Class I only (Class III requires in vivo BE)'
contradictions.skill_2_instruction: skill-eu-gmp-analysis.md Phase 4 Gap #13: 'EMA does not accept Class III biowaivers'
contradictions.skill_3_instruction: skill-multi-jurisdiction.md: 'FDA BCS Class III: No (restricted); EMA: Yes (with conditions)'
contradictions.resolution: skill-eu-gmp-analysis.md Gap #13 says 'EMA does not accept Class III biowaivers' but skill-multi-jurisdiction.md says EMA DOES accept them with conditions. The eu-gmp skill has the WRONG statement. EMA guideline CPMP/EWP/QWP/1401/98 Rev.1 explicitly allows BCS Class III biowaivers under specific dissolution conditions.
contradictions.risk: MEDIUM — skill-eu-gmp-analysis.md Gap #13 would give incorrect guidance. The statement should read 'EMA accepts BCS Class III biowaivers (with conditions)' not 'does not accept'

contradictions.skill_1_instruction: skill-safety-pharmacology.md Step 3A: 'If protein binding <1% experimentally, use 1% as floor'
contradictions.skill_2_instruction: skill-safety-reporting.md Section 7.4: 'Unbound fraction floor of 1% applied if measured < 1%'
contradictions.resolution: Identical requirement stated in both skills. No contradiction — consistent E14/S7B Q&A guidance.

contradictions.topic: Severity/scoring scales are inconsistent across skills

detail.skill-clinical-design.md: 5-level: Critical(0) / Major(1) / Moderate(2) / Minor(3) / Observation(4) with weighted % scoring
detail.skill-quality-systems.md: 4-level: Compliant(3) / Partially Compliant(2) / Deficient(1) / Not Addressed(0)
detail.skill-special-populations.md: 4-level: Compliant(4) / Minor gaps(3) / Significant gaps(2) / Non-compliant(1)
detail.skill-canada-regulatory.md: 3-level: Critical Gaps / High Priority Gaps / Canada-Specific Flags

contradictions.resolution: These are different skills assessing different things, so some variation is expected. However, when a cross-domain review aggregates findings from multiple skills, inconsistent scales create confusion.
contradictions.risk: LOW — Problematic only when combining outputs from multiple skills into a unified report

contradictions.topic: BCS Class III biowaiver statement is incorrect
contradictions.detail: Phase 4 Gap Analysis table, row #13 states: 'BCS Class III biowaiver attempted — EMA does not accept Class III biowaivers — Conduct BE study'. This is factually wrong. EMA guideline CPMP/EWP/QWP/1401/98 Rev.1 (2010) explicitly allows BCS Class III biowaivers when the product dissolves very rapidly (>=85% in 15 min) at all three pH values and excipients are qualitatively and quantitatively the same. The same-skill regulatory-compliance.md correctly identifies this as an FDA-vs-EMA divergence. The multi-jurisdiction skill also correctly states EMA accepts Class III.
contradictions.remediation: Change row #13 to: 'BCS Class III biowaiver attempted — EMA accepts Class III biowaivers under specific conditions (very rapid dissolution, Q/Q same excipients) per CPMP/EWP/QWP/1401/98 Rev.1 — Verify dissolution and excipient similarity requirements met'

contradictions.detail: skill-nonclinical-review.md STEP 3 covers S7A/S7B core battery and QT assessment in ~50 lines. skill-safety-pharmacology.md covers the same material in ~330 lines with much greater depth (hERG best practices, follow-up study triggers, integrated risk assessment framework, timing alignment). Neither skill references the other or explains when to use which.
contradictions.remediation: Add a cross-reference in skill-nonclinical-review.md STEP 3: 'For detailed S7A/S7B evaluation, invoke skill-safety-pharmacology.md' and reduce the nonclinical skill's SP section to a summary checklist with clear handoff.

contradictions.topic: PSUR/PBRER evaluation duplicated across ICH and EU-specific skills
contradictions.detail: skill-safety-reporting.md Section 4 covers PBRER evaluation per ICH E2C(R2) with 20 structural sections and detailed signal/benefit-risk assessment. skill-eu-pharmacovigilance.md Section 4 covers PSUR per GVP Module VII with EU regional appendix requirements. Both cover the same document type but from different regulatory perspectives. No cross-reference between them.
contradictions.remediation: Add cross-references. skill-safety-reporting.md should note: 'For EU-specific PSUR requirements (PRAC assessment, EU regional appendix), see skill-eu-pharmacovigilance.md Section 4.' skill-eu-pharmacovigilance.md should note: 'For ICH E2C(R2) structural requirements of the PSUR/PBRER, see skill-safety-reporting.md Section 4.'

contradictions.topic: EU requirements split across three skills with partial overlap
contradictions.detail: All three v3 EU skills cover overlapping EU topics: (1) QPPV requirement appears in both skill-eu-regulatory.md (Step 5) and skill-eu-pharmacovigilance.md (Section 1). (2) RMP requirement appears in skill-eu-regulatory.md (Step 2) and skill-eu-pharmacovigilance.md (Section 3). (3) Manufacturing/GMP appears in skill-eu-regulatory.md (Step 4) and skill-eu-gmp-analysis.md (all phases). None reference each other.
contradictions.remediation: Add a delineation note to each: eu-regulatory = legislative framework and pathway selection; eu-gmp-analysis = Module 3 CMC deep dive; eu-pharmacovigilance = GVP-based PV system evaluation. Add cross-references where topics overlap.

rule_id_verification.summary: 6 of 13 JSON rule files have zero rule IDs. 3 files have inflated metadata counts that do not match actual rule counts.

files_with_ids.discrepancy: Metadata overcounts by 11; SKILL.md correctly states 136 via domain breakdown
files_with_ids.coverage: COMPLETE — all ranges sequential with no gaps

files_with_ids.note: SKILL.md uses E9-R1-001 format; JSON uses E9R1-001 (cosmetic mismatch only)

files_with_ids.discrepancy: CRITICAL — metadata total_rules=412 but only 132 rule objects exist. The 412 appears to come from obligation_summary counts being mistaken for rule counts.
files_with_ids.coverage: All existing ranges are complete; the shortfall is in total count, not in gaps

files_with_ids.discrepancy: N/A — SKILL.md does not claim a count
CRITICAL — metadata claims 412 but only 139 requirement objects exist (~66% shortfall)
files_with_ids.coverage: All existing ranges complete; total count inflated
Good but missing GVP Modules III, IV, VI, X-XIV

files_with_ids.discrepancy: Only significant_change_criteria section has IDs (SC-1 to SC-6); ~95% of file content has no IDs

rule_id_verification.regulatory_requirements_json_note: US FDA section has zero IDs; EMA and PMDA sections have 22 IDs total

gaps_topics_in_json_not_in_skill.json_has: gvp-unique-requirements.json exists as a separate file with EU-unique PV requirements

gaps_topics_in_json_not_in_skill.skill_coverage: skill-nonclinical-review.md mentions S13 in STEP 1 table but has no detailed evaluation checklist for ONTs (noted as 'when finalized')
skill-cross-domain.md mentions M13C only briefly (1 rule noted) but does not provide detailed evaluation guidance for HVD/NTI scenarios
